home.lead: Meridian's complete brand system — from strategy to application. One single source of truth, in six chapters.
home.howBody: Each chapter pairs the rule, its rationale, and examples of correct and incorrect use. Move between chapters from the top menu, jump to any section from this index or the sub-section bar on each page, and switch language with the ES/EN toggle.

strategic.title: Why the brand exists
strategic.lead: Who we are and how we differ — the base every visual and verbal decision flows from.

voice.title: How Meridian sounds
voice.lead: A single verbal personality, consistent in every word and adaptable to every context.

visual.title: How Meridian looks
visual.lead: The complete visual system: logo, color, typography, and the elements that build recognition.

applications.title: The brand in the real world
applications.lead: The system applied across every surface, with specs and ready-to-use templates.

resources.title: Assets, legal & governance
resources.lead: Everything to use and maintain the brand: downloads, rights, ownership, and versioning.

purpose.mission: The why (purpose), the where (vision), the what today (mission), and the how (values).
purpose.story: The origin and narrative that give the brand context and authenticity.
purpose.positioning: The place Meridian holds in the customer's mind and how it stands apart.
purpose.value-prop: The core value the brand promises and why it matters.
purpose.audience: Who we speak to: segments, needs, and representative profiles.
purpose.personality: The human traits and archetype that define the brand's character.
purpose.verbal-foundations: Why the voice exists and how it anchors to strategy and audience.
purpose.voice-tone: Voice is constant; tone adapts to context. The key distinction.
purpose.writing-principles: Each personality trait translated into actionable writing techniques.
purpose.tone-flexing: How tone flexes with the reader's mood and journey stage.
purpose.messaging: Value proposition, key messages, and proof: what to say and in what order.
purpose.naming: Rules for product names, the tagline, and calls to action.
purpose.grammar: Punctuation, capitalization, numbers, and conventions that unify writing.
purpose.formats: How dates, times, numbers, currency, and units are written per language, in data and in product.
purpose.terminology: Preferred lexicon, product terms, and words we never use.
purpose.inclusive: Writing for everyone with clear, respectful, and legible language.
purpose.examples: Rewrites and correct/incorrect pairs that make the voice tangible.
purpose.channels: How the brand sounds across social, email, product, support, and legal.
purpose.ai-voice: Adjectives, guardrails, and a prompt-ready context block for AI.
purpose.logo: The primary logotype and all its approved variants.
purpose.logo-usage: Clear space, minimum size, valid backgrounds, and file formats.
purpose.logo-misuse: Forbidden manipulations: stretching, recoloring, rotating, adding effects.
purpose.color: Primary, secondary, and neutral colors with HEX/RGB/CMYK/Pantone values.
purpose.typography: Families, type scale, weights, and hierarchy for screen and print.
purpose.accessibility: WCAG ratios, safe color pairings, and minimum legible sizes.
purpose.darkmode: How to implement consistent, accessible dark mode on the web: surfaces, contrast, what flips, and the technical pattern.
purpose.iconography: Style, construction grid, stroke, and library of the icon system.
purpose.imagery: Style, treatment, and criteria for the brand's photography.
purpose.illustration: Principles, color, and composition of the proprietary illustration language.
purpose.graphic-elements: Patterns, shapes, and brand devices that extend recognition.
purpose.grid: Print, web, and app grids, margins, and the spacing scale.
purpose.motion: Motion principles, easing, duration, and logo animation.
purpose.dataviz: Color, chart types, and labeling for on-brand, legible data.
purpose.sonic: Audio logo, music style, and interface sounds of the brand.
purpose.digital: Identity behavior on screen, web, and favicons/PWA.
purpose.app-ui: Applying the brand inside the product interface.
purpose.app-icons: iOS/Android icon matrix and App Store / Play Store assets.
purpose.social: Per-platform dimensions: avatar, banner, post, and story.
purpose.email: Email templates and a standardized signature.
purpose.advertising: Banners (IAB sizes), display, and campaign pieces.
purpose.packaging: Applying the brand to packaging and labeling.
purpose.signage: Signage and wayfinding in physical spaces.
purpose.environmental: Environment design, stands, and retail experience.
purpose.cobranding: Lockups with partner brands, hierarchy, and approval rules.
purpose.assets: Logos, fonts, palettes, and kits ready to download.
purpose.governance: Brand owner, approval workflow, and contact.

viz.clearspaceNote: The exclusion zone equals the height of the symbol (1×) on all four sides.
viz.iconsNote: 2 px stroke on a 24 px grid, 2 px corners. Outline style, single family.
viz.illNote: Clean geometry, brand palette, and consistent stroke.
viz.motionNote: Ease-out entrances, 200–320 ms. Motion guides, it doesn't decorate.
viz.chartNote: Series mapped to the brand tonal scale; never rely on color alone.
viz.advNote: Demonstrative placeholder · advanced section. Filled when the brand activates this touchpoint.
viz.darkNote: In dark mode the accent shifts to brand-400 (#4F6FEE) to keep AA contrast; light mode keeps Cobalt #2348E0. Swatches and the palette always show the literal color — they never invert.

p3.missionText: Help every team create with clarity and confidence.
p3.visionText: Be the reference point that aligns brand, product, and communication.
p3.v1d: We say what matters, without noise.
p3.posText: For fast-growing teams, Meridian is the brand system that keeps everything aligned — from strategy to the last application.
p3.vpText: One single source of truth for the brand: less friction, more consistency.
p3.promiseText: Follow the guide and it always looks and sounds like Meridian.
p3.p1need: Scale the brand without losing consistency.
p3.p1quote: I need anyone on the team to create on-brand.
p3.p2quote: Let the templates do the heavy lifting.
p3.archetypeText: Meridian guides and supports: expert, clear, and reliable, never the protagonist.
p3.voiceDef: Who we are when we write: clear, confident, human. It doesn't change.
p3.toneDef: How we adapt to context and the reader's state of mind. It changes.
p3.t2ex: “This is the best option for you.”
p3.baOn: “Something went wrong while saving. Try again in a moment.”
p3.baOff: “ERROR 500: the request could not be processed by the server.”
p3.msgL1t: One source of truth · Ready-made templates · Consistency at scale.
p3.aiNote: Block ready to paste into your AI system prompt.
p3.adNote: Standard IAB sizes for display and campaigns.
p3.tmNote: Meridian™ is a registered trademark. Use ™ on first use and ® once registered. Do not alter the logotype.
p3.approvalsVal: Request a review before publishing new pieces.
p3.purposeText: We exist so any team can create with the consistency of a great brand.
p3.storyText: Meridian was born from a simple idea: consistency shouldn't depend on one person. What began as an internal guide is now a complete system.
p3.aaText: Growing teams that value speed and consistency; they want to look professional without slowing down.
p3.pbNeed: Protect the brand and ensure consistency.
p3.pbQuote: Every piece must look like us.
p3.fallbackNote: If the brand fonts don't load, a metric-adjusted system stack (size-adjust) is used to avoid layout shift (CLS).
p3.gr1c: Capitalize only the first word and proper nouns.
p3.gr3c: The subject acts; avoid the passive.
p3.gr3e: “We publish your brand,” not “Your brand was published.”
p3.gr4c: Zero to nine in words; 10+ as numerals.
p3.gr5c: No ALL CAPS; use weight to highlight.

fmt.noteAmbiguous: Never use all-numeric dates like 03/04/2026 across locales — they're ambiguous (April 3 or March 4?). Use a written month or ISO 8601.
fmt.noteMachine: In web and apps, don't hand-format: store in ISO 8601 and display with Intl.DateTimeFormat / Intl.NumberFormat (Unicode CLDR/ICU) per locale.

dm.e1: Define semantic tokens (background, surface, text, accent) and let each theme resolve them. The same component works in both with no rewrite.
dm.e2: Pure black (#000) vibrates against text, tires the eyes and hides elevation. Use #0E1116 (Ink) as the base.
dm.e3: On dark, shadows barely register; depth comes from lightening elevated surfaces, not from shadow.
dm.e4: Saturated cobalt loses contrast and vibrates on dark. Lift it to #4F6FEE (fills) and #7E99F6 (text/links, ≥4.5:1).
dm.e5: Pure white causes halation: letters seem to glow and tire the eye. Use an off-white (#F7F9FC) on the dark base.
dm.e6: WCAG AA (4.5:1 text, 3:1 UI) is no dark-mode exemption: re-measure per theme, never assume the light pass.
dm.surfNote: Each level lightens: elevation reads through lightness, not shadow (shadows barely register on dark).
dm.accentNote: On dark the accent shifts to brand-400 #4F6FEE and text/links to #7E99F6 (≥4.5:1). Cobalt #2348E0 is reserved for fills/backgrounds, not text on dark.
dm.contrastNote: WCAG AA applies in both themes (4.5:1 text, 3:1 large/UI) and is re-measured per theme. Never #FFF on #000.
dm.flip3: The logo uses its reversed variant
dm.webNote: Three-state system | light | dark, persisted, with a matchMedia listener; light-dark() as progressive enhancement.
dm.preview: Same interface, two themes. The system re-maps roles, it doesn't invert.
